Corollary 2.13. \(L^{p}[a, b]\) is closed under taking absolute values and maximums.

If \(\{ c_{k} \}\) in \(C[a, b]\) is a Cauchy sequence in \(L^{p}[a, b]\)-norm, representing \(\phi\) in \(L^{p}[a, b]\text{,}\) then \(\{ |c_{k}| \}\) in \(C[a, b]\) is a Cauchy sequence in \(L^{p}[a, b]\)-norm whose realization is given by \(|\phi (x)|\text{.}\)
If \(\phi, \psi\in L^{p}[a, b]\text{,}\) then \(\max\{\phi, \psi\} :=(\phi + \psi + |\phi - \psi||)/2 \in L^{p}[a, b]\) and \(\min\{\phi, \psi\} :=(\phi + \psi - |\phi - \psi|)/2 \in L^{p}[a, b]\text{.}\)
in-context
